results.title: MTAR Technologies sees its largest single-day drop ever.
results.summary: MTAR Technologies sees its largest single-day drop ever.

results.title:
MTAR Tech shares drop 11% after Crusoe Energy halts a 1.8 GW project tied to key client Bloom Energy.
MTAR derives 55-65% revenue from Bloom; concerns rise over order inflows. Stock down 17% in 5 sessions.
results.summary:
MTAR Tech shares drop 11% after Crusoe Energy halts a 1.8 GW project tied to key client Bloom Energy.
MTAR derives 55-65% revenue from Bloom; concerns rise over order inflows. Stock down 17% in 5 sessions.

results.title:
MTAR Tech shares fell 8% intraday, hitting Rs. 6,470 before marginally recovering. The stock is down from its 52-week high of Rs. 8,449.50.
Analysts attribute the decline to customer concentration concerns but remain optimistic about its long-term prospects in niche sectors.
results.summary:
MTAR Tech shares fell 8% intraday, hitting Rs. 6,470 before marginally recovering. The stock is down from its 52-week high of Rs. 8,449.50.
Analysts attribute the decline to customer concentration concerns but remain optimistic about its long-term prospects in niche sectors.

results.title:
Mtar Tech faces near-term overhang as Bloom Energy project hits issues.
Operational delays risk execution timeline and revenue recognition for the company.
results.summary:

results.title:
MTAR Technologies' FY27 order book growth and data center sector expansion are in focus.
The firm is recognized for precision engineering across aerospace, defense, and clean energy.

results.title:
Indian industrial firms like Sterlite Tech, HFCL, and MTAR see massive gains amid global AI capex boom.
Data center investments by giants like Reliance, AdaniConnex, and Amazon fuel India's industrial supply chain growth.
results.summary:

results.title:
Defence stocks, including IdeaForge, Apollo Micro, MTAR, Paras Defence, and CFF Fluid Control, saw revenue growth up to 605% YoY in Q4 FY26.
Key drivers include rising indigenous manufacturing, higher defence spending, and strong order inflows, boosting financial performance and investor interest.
results.summary:
Defence stocks, including IdeaForge, Apollo Micro, MTAR, Paras Defence, and CFF Fluid Control, saw revenue growth up to 605% YoY in Q4 FY26.
Key drivers include rising indigenous manufacturing, higher defence spending, and strong order inflows, boosting financial performance and investor interest.

results.title: MTAR Tech shares surged 24% over three sessions amid increased buying interest.
results.summary: MTAR Tech shares surged 24% over three sessions amid increased buying interest.

results.title: MTAR Technologies wins an international order worth ₹467 crore.

results.title:
MTAR Technologies secures ₹467.3 crore (USD 48.7M) overseas orders.
Deliveries for the orders are scheduled by March and June 2027.
